mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the unemployment rate indicates improving labor market conditions that boost consumer spending expectations and corporate revenue forecasts, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in more than 50% of months when UNRATE declined month-over-month during the walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9%, elevated labor slack suppresses wage growth and consumer demand, compressing profit margins and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 9%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and nonfarm payrolls signals a broad-based demand recovery that outpaces supply constraints, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint z-score of RSAFS and PAYEMS fails to produce a rank correlation above 0.05 with 20-day forward returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods when the federal funds rate is held below 1%, further month-over-month declines in the rate signal additional unconventional monetary accommodation that lowers the equity risk premium, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 1%, months with rate declines do not produce statistically significantly positive 5-day returns compared to months with stable or rising rates (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth signals rising manufacturing profitability and capacity utilization, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month increases in INDPRO coincide with negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations during the test period.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP indicates expanding aggregate demand that supports top-line revenue growth across the corporate sector, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive quarter-over-quarter GDPC1 growth is followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of quarterly observations.

mechanism_narrative: Year-over-year consumer price inflation exceeding 2% erodes real corporate earnings and prompts anticipation of monetary tightening, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered months when CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 2%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in housing starts and retail sales reflect broadening consumer and investment demand that reduces recession probability, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The combination of rising HOUST and RSAFS fails to predict positive 5-day returns with an accuracy above 55% over any 2-year rolling window.
